irish: Ní neart go cur le chéile.
english: There is no strength without unity.
literal: There is no strength until you put it together.

irish: Is fearr Gaeilge briste ná Béarla cliste.
english: Broken Irish is better than clever English.
literal: Broken Irish is better than clever English.

irish: Ar scáth a chéile a mhaireann na daoine.
english: People live in each other's shelter.
literal: In the shadow of each other people live.

irish: Ní bhíonn an rath ach mar a mbíonn an smacht.
english: There is no prosperity without discipline.
literal: Prosperity only exists where there is order.

irish: Is maith an t-anlann an t-ocras.
english: Hunger is a good sauce.
literal: Hunger is good seasoning.

irish: Mol an óige agus tiocfaidh sí.
english: Praise the young and they will flourish.
literal: Praise the young and they will come.

irish: Ní hé lá na gaoithe lá na scolb.
english: A windy day is not the day for thatching.
literal: The day of the wind is not the day for scallops.

irish: Bíonn súil le muir ach ní bhíonn súil le tír.
english: There is hope from the sea but not from the grave.
literal: There is hope from the sea but no hope from the land.

irish: Is fearr rith maith ná drochsheasamh.
english: A good run is better than a bad stand.
literal: A good run is better than a bad standing.

irish: Maireann croí éadrom i bhfad.
english: A light heart lives long.
literal: A light heart lives long.

irish: An rud is annamh is iontach.
english: What is seldom is wonderful.
literal: The thing that is rare is wonderful.

irish: Ná déan nós agus ná bris nós.
english: Don't make a habit and don't break a habit.
literal: Don't make a custom and don't break a custom.

irish: Is minic a bhris béal duine a shrón.
english: Many a time a man's mouth broke his nose.
literal: Often a person's mouth broke their nose.

irish: Filleann an feall ar an bhfeallaire.
english: Treachery returns to the traitor.
literal: The treachery returns to the traitor.

irish: Giorraíonn beirt bóthar.
english: Two shorten the road.
literal: Two people shorten a road.

irish: Is fada an bóthar nach mbíonn casadh ann.
english: It's a long road that has no turning.
literal: It is a long road that has no turn in it.

irish: Nuair a bhíonn an cat amuigh, bíonn na lucha ag rince.
english: When the cat is out, the mice dance.
literal: When the cat is outside, the mice are dancing.

irish: Is treise tuile ná trua.
english: A flood is stronger than pity.
literal: Flood is stronger than pity.

irish: Aithníonn ciaróg ciaróg eile.
english: A beetle recognises another beetle.
literal: A beetle recognises another beetle.

irish: Ní féidir leat suí ar dhá chapall ag an am céanna.
english: You cannot sit on two horses at the same time.
literal: You cannot sit on two horses at the same time.

irish: Is fearr an tsláinte ná na táinte.
english: Health is better than wealth.
literal: Health is better than herds.

irish: Bíonn gach tosach lag.
english: Every beginning is weak.
literal: Every beginning is weak.

irish: Is maith an scáthán súil carad.
english: A friend's eye is a good mirror.
literal: A friend's eye is a good mirror.

irish: Ná bí ar an gcéad le nuacht a thabhairt.
english: Don't be first to bring news.
literal: Don't be the first to bring news.

irish: Castar na daoine ar a chéile ach ní castar na cnoic.
english: People meet but hills do not.
literal: People are turned towards each other but hills are not turned.

irish: Ní thagann ciall roimh aois.
english: Wisdom doesn't come before age.
literal: Sense does not come before age.

irish: Bíonn adharca fada ar na ba thar lear.
english: Far-away cows have long horns.
literal: Cows overseas have long horns.

irish: Ní féidir an t-arán a ithe agus a bheith agat.
english: You cannot eat your bread and have it.
literal: You cannot eat the bread and have it.

irish: Is mó cleas ná neart.
english: Cleverness is more than strength.
literal: There is more trick than strength.

irish: Tugann gach éan grá dá nead féin.
english: Every bird loves its own nest.
literal: Every bird gives love to its own nest.

irish: Ná cuir do mhéar idir an adhmad agus an rinn.
english: Don't put your finger between the wood and the blade.
literal: Don't put your finger between the wood and the point.

irish: Is buaine bladhm ná brí.
english: A blaze lasts longer than substance.
literal: A blaze is more lasting than substance.

irish: Ní chuireann an saol ach cuid dó féin air féin.
english: The world only puts its own share on itself.
literal: The world only puts some of itself on itself.

irish: Beatha duine a thoil.
english: A person's life is their will.
literal: A person's life is their desire.

irish: Is maith an rud iad cúig mhinicíochtaí.
english: The five senses are a good thing.
literal: Five frequencies are a good thing.

irish: Ná bris do loirgín ar stól nach bhfuil i do shlí.
english: Don't break your shin on a stool that isn't in your way.
literal: Don't break your shin on a stool not in your path.

irish: Is fusa caint ná obair.
english: Talk is easier than work.
literal: Talk is easier than work.

irish: Ní maith an gaoith nach séideann do dhuine éigin.
english: It's an ill wind that blows no one good.
literal: It is not a good wind that blows for no one.

irish: Éist le fuaim na habhann agus gheobhaidh tú breac.
english: Listen to the sound of the river and you will catch a trout.
literal: Listen to the sound of the river and you will get a trout.

irish: Ná déan deifir, déan é ceart.
english: Don't hurry, do it right.
literal: Don't make haste, do it right.

irish: Is fearr focal sa chúirt ná punt sa sparán.
english: A word in court is worth more than a pound in a purse.
literal: A word in court is better than a pound in a purse.

irish: Ní thagann gach maidin ach uair amháin.
english: Every morning comes only once.
literal: Every morning only comes once.

irish: Ní mar a shíltear a bhítear.
english: Things are not always as they seem.
literal: It is not as one thinks that one is.

irish: Is maith an bhail ar an gcaint é.
english: Actions speak louder than words.
literal: Good fortune comes from action not words.

irish: Ní fheicfidh súil leath oiread le dhá shúil.
english: One eye sees less than two.
literal: One eye won't see half as much as two eyes.

irish: An rud nach gcuala tú, ná habair é.
english: Don't repeat what you haven't heard.
literal: The thing you did not hear, don't say it.

irish: Ní éiríonn an ghrian ar son amadáin amháin.
english: The sun does not rise for one fool alone.
literal: The sun does not rise for the sake of one fool.

irish: Ní bhíonn an t-ádh i gcónaí ar an gcaor chéanna.
english: Luck is not always on the same bush.
literal: Luck is not always on the same berry bush.

irish: Is fearr cara sa chúirt ná punt sa sparán.
english: A friend in court is better than a pound in a purse.
literal: A friend in court is better than a pound in a purse.

irish: Imíonn na bliana is fanann na laethanta.
english: The years go but the days remain.
literal: The years go away and the days stay.

irish: Is minic a bhí cú mall sona.
english: A slow hound was often lucky.
literal: Often the slow hound was lucky.

irish: Ná caith do chuid airgid ar rud nach bhfuil agat.
english: Don't spend money you don't have.
literal: Don't spend your money on a thing you don't have.

irish: Is cosúil le hobair mhaith an dua mór.
english: Great effort looks like good work.
literal: Great effort is like good work.

irish: Is cuma leis an gcat cé acu a bhíonn sé ar an gclár nó faoin gclár.
english: The cat doesn't mind whether it's on or under the shelf.
literal: The cat doesn't care whether it is on the board or under the board.

irish: Níor dhún Dia doras riamh nar oscail sé ceann eile.
english: God never closed one door without opening another.
literal: God never closed a door without opening another one.

irish: Is glas iad na cnoic i bhfad uainn.
english: The hills far away are green.
literal: The hills that are far from us are green.

irish: Bíonn caora dhubh ar an tréad is gile.
english: There is a black sheep in the whitest flock.
literal: A black sheep is in the brightest flock.

irish: Is fearr an tsláinte ná an táinte.
english: Good health is better than wealth.
literal: Health is better than herds of cattle.

irish: Ceol gan amhrán mar mháistir gan scoil.
english: Music without song is like a master without a school.
literal: Music without song is like a master without a school.

irish: Is fearr greim de ghiorria ná dhá ghreim de chat.
english: A taste of hare is better than two tastes of cat.
literal: A bite of hare is better than two bites of cat.

irish: Cuir uait é nó cuir leat é.
english: Either put it from you or take it with you.
literal: Put it from you or put it with you.

irish: Gach tosach lag is gach deireadh láidir.
english: Every beginning is weak and every ending is strong.
literal: Every beginning weak and every end strong.

irish: Ná lig do rún le fear óg ná le páiste.
english: Don't share your secret with a young man or a child.
literal: Don't let your secret to a young man or to a child.

irish: Is iomaí cor sa tsaol.
english: Life has many twists.
literal: There are many turns in life.

irish: Nuair is treise an cat, tosaíonn sé ag ithe.
english: When the cat is strongest it begins to eat.
literal: When the cat is strongest, it starts eating.

irish: Is fearr bheith i measc na ndaoine ná bheith aonarach.
english: It is better to be among people than to be alone.
literal: It is better to be among the people than to be alone.

irish: Caith do bholgam is ná bain torann as.
english: Take your mouthful and make no noise about it.
literal: Throw your mouthful and make no noise from it.

irish: Ní fhanann trá le fear mall.
english: The ebb tide waits for no one.
literal: The ebb tide does not wait for a slow man.

irish: Cuir síoda ar ghabhar is is gabhar i gcónaí é.
english: Put silk on a goat and it's still a goat.
literal: Put silk on a goat and it is a goat still.

irish: Bíonn an rath i gceann na mbeart.
english: Luck is at the head of deeds.
literal: Prosperity is at the head of deeds.

irish: Tuar drochaimsire an ghrian bheith dearg tráthnóna.
english: A red sun at evening foretells bad weather.
literal: A red sun in the evening is an omen of bad weather.

irish: Ná caith do chuid ama ag caint faoin obair, déan í.
english: Don't waste your time talking about work, do it.
literal: Don't spend your time talking about the work, do it.

irish: Creid ach fíoraigh.
english: Believe but verify.
literal: Believe but confirm.

irish: Ní thuigeann an sách an seang.
english: The well-fed does not understand the hungry.
literal: The satiated does not understand the lean.

irish: Is cóir do gach duine a bhróg féin a chaitheamh.
english: Everyone should wear their own shoe.
literal: It is right for every person to wear their own shoe.

irish: Níl aon tintéan mar do thintéan féin.
english: There's no fireside like your own fireside.
literal: There is no fireside like your own fireside.

irish: Bíodh foighne agat agus gheobhaidh tú do mhian.
english: Have patience and you will get your desire.
literal: Have patience and you will get your wish.

irish: Tá sé chomh fada le Lá Samhraidh gan aon oíche.
english: It is as long as a summer's day without night.
literal: It is as long as a summer's day without any night.

irish: Is minic a bhíonn an fhírinne searbh.
english: The truth is often bitter.
literal: The truth is often bitter.

irish: Más maith leat dul i bhfad, ná gabh leat do lámh fholamh.
english: If you want to go far, don't go empty-handed.
literal: If you like to go far, don't take an empty hand with you.

irish: Bíonn an duine go minic mar a bhíonn a chuideachta.
english: A person is often like the company they keep.
literal: A person is often as their company is.

irish: Aithnítear cara i gcruachás.
english: A friend is known in hardship.
literal: A friend is recognized in difficulty.

irish: Is maith an rud é an t-airgead a bheith agat ach níl maith dá laghad ann mura gcaitheann tú é.
english: Money is good to have but worthless if you don't spend it.
literal: Money is a good thing to have but it's no use if you don't spend it.

irish: Ní mar a cheaptar a tharlaíonn.
english: Things don't always happen as expected.
literal: It doesn't happen as it is thought.

irish: Gach uile dhuine ar a lámh féin.
english: Every man for himself.
literal: Every person on their own hand.

irish: Más fada an lá tagann an tráthnóna.
english: However long the day, the evening comes.
literal: Even if the day is long, the evening comes.

irish: Ní dhíolann dearmad fiacha.
english: Forgetting doesn't pay debts.
literal: Forgetting does not pay debts.

irish: Fógraíonn an éan a theacht féin.
english: A bird announces its own arrival.
literal: The bird announces its own coming.

irish: Is fearr troid mhaith ná aon drochshíocháin.
english: A good fight is better than a bad peace.
literal: A good fight is better than any bad peace.

irish: Ní bhíonn an fear maith gan a locht.
english: Even a good man has his faults.
literal: The good man is not without his fault.

irish: Déan beagán agus déan é maith.
english: Do little and do it well.
literal: Do a little and do it well.

irish: Bíonn an saol ag éirí níos fearr i gcónaí.
english: Life is always getting better.
literal: Life is always getting better.

irish: Inis dom cé leis a mbíonn tú is inseoidh mé duit cé thú féin.
english: Tell me who you walk with and I'll tell you who you are.
literal: Tell me who you are with and I will tell you who you yourself are.

irish: Is fearr deireadh maith ná tús maith.
english: A good ending is better than a good beginning.
literal: A good end is better than a good start.

irish: Ní féidir an ghaoth a choinneáil i bpóca.
english: You cannot keep the wind in a pocket.
literal: The wind cannot be kept in a pocket.

irish: An té nach gcuireann san earrach ní bhaineann sé san fhómhar.
english: He who does not sow in spring will not reap in autumn.
literal: The one who does not sow in spring does not reap in autumn.

irish: Ná bris an chiúin go mbrisfear ort í.
english: Don't break the peace until it's broken upon you.
literal: Don't break the peace until it is broken on you.

irish: Más maith leat an tsíocháin, ullmhaigh don chogadh.
english: If you want peace, prepare for war.
literal: If you like peace, prepare for war.

irish: Is leor nod don eolach.
english: A word to the wise is enough.
literal: A nod is enough for the knowledgeable person.

irish: Is fearr an tsimplíocht ná an chlíste.
english: Simplicity is better than cleverness.
literal: Simplicity is better than cleverness.

irish: Ná cuir an méar sa tine mura mbeidh tú sásta le dó.
english: Don't put your finger in the fire unless you're willing to be burnt.
literal: Don't put the finger in the fire if you won't be happy with a burn.

irish: Bíonn siúlach scéalach.
english: A traveller has many tales.
literal: One who travels is full of stories.

irish: Má tá an hata ort caith é.
english: If the hat fits, wear it.
literal: If the hat is on you, wear it.

irish: Cuireann áthas croí áilleacht ar an aghaidh.
english: A joyful heart makes a beautiful face.
literal: Joy of heart puts beauty on the face.

irish: Is é an spéir an teorainn.
english: The sky is the limit.
literal: The sky is the boundary.

irish: Tús maith leath na hoibre.
english: A good start is half the work.
literal: A good beginning is half the work.

irish: Ní hionann inniu agus inné.
english: Today is not the same as yesterday.
literal: Today and yesterday are not the same.

irish: Ná téigh i muinín mada gan fhiacail.
english: Don't trust a dog without teeth.
literal: Don't rely on a dog without teeth.

irish: Is fearr cosc ná leigheas.
english: Prevention is better than cure.
literal: Prevention is better than remedy.

irish: An rud a bhíonn sa chnámh, bíonn sé sa tsmior.
english: What's in the bone is in the marrow.
literal: What is in the bone is in the marrow.

irish: Más é do thoil é, is é do cheol é.
english: What you desire is your music.
literal: If it is your will, it is your music.

irish: Ná déan obair an lae amáraigh inniu.
english: Don't do tomorrow's work today.
literal: Don't do tomorrow's work today.

irish: Bíonn gach duine lách go dtéann bó ina gharraí.
english: Everyone is pleasant until a cow gets into their garden.
literal: Everyone is nice until a cow goes into their garden.

irish: Is maith an t-anlann an t-ocras.
english: Hunger is a good sauce.
literal: Hunger is good seasoning.

irish: Ní thiocfaidh an lá nach dtiocfaidh a oíche féin.
english: There is no day that won't bring its own night.
literal: No day will come that won't bring its own night.

irish: Nuair a bhíonn an fhírinne i gceist, ná bíodh eagla ort.
english: When truth is at stake, have no fear.
literal: When truth is involved, don't be afraid.

irish: Ní bhíonn bua gan donas.
english: There is no victory without some loss.
literal: There is no victory without misfortune.

irish: Is iomaí malairt intinne a tharlaíonn idir lámha.
english: Many a change of mind happens in the doing.
literal: Many a change of mind happens between the hands.

irish: Bíonn an saol mar shnámh in aghaidh easa.
english: Life is like swimming against a waterfall.
literal: Life is like swimming against a waterfall.

irish: Is buaine clú ná saol.
english: Reputation outlasts life.
literal: Fame is more lasting than life.

irish: Ní thagann feabhas ar obair leis an deifir.
english: Haste does not improve the work.
literal: Improvement in work doesn't come with haste.

irish: Ní féidir leis an mbeach mil a dhéanamh gan bláthanna.
english: The bee cannot make honey without flowers.
literal: The bee cannot make honey without flowers.

irish: Is maith an giolla an t-am.
english: Time is a good servant.
literal: Time is a good helper.

irish: Beir ar an deis agus ná lig uait í.
english: Seize the opportunity and don't let it go.
literal: Take the opportunity and don't let it from you.

irish: Ná bíodh do theanga faoi do chrios.
english: Don't let your tongue be under your belt.
literal: Don't have your tongue under your belt.

irish: An rud nach bhfuil i ndán duit, ní bhfaighidh tú é.
english: What is not fated for you, you will not get.
literal: The thing not destined for you, you will not get.

irish: Is leigheas ar gach olc an foighne.
english: Patience is a cure for every ill.
literal: Patience is a cure for every evil.

irish: Scéal ar bith níos fearr ná drochscéal.
english: No news is better than bad news.
literal: No story is better than a bad story.

irish: Ní gach uile lá a bhíonn an fharraige socair.
english: Not every day is the sea calm.
literal: Not every day is the sea at rest.

irish: Bíonn an mhaith agus an t-olc ag dul le chéile.
english: Good and evil go together.
literal: Good and evil go with each other.

irish: Is mó rud le foghlaim ná mar is féidir a mhúineadh.
english: There is more to learn than can ever be taught.
literal: There are more things to learn than can be taught.

irish: Féach sular léime tú.
english: Look before you leap.
literal: Look before you jump.

irish: Is maith an ceol an obair.
english: Work is good music.
literal: Work is good music.

irish: Ná déan cúl le cine.
english: Don't turn your back on your own people.
literal: Don't turn a back to your kin.

irish: Is fearr beagán den ghaol ná mórán den charthanas.
english: A little kinship is better than much charity.
literal: A little of kinship is better than a lot of charity.

irish: Más glas iad na cnoic i bhfad uait, is glas do gort féin freisin.
english: If the distant hills are green, so too is your own field.
literal: If the hills far from you are green, your own field is green too.

irish: Is é an duine ciallmhar nach ndéanann gach rud is féidir leis a dhéanamh.
english: A wise person does not do everything they are capable of.
literal: The sensible person is who does not do all they can do.

irish: Is cuma faoin gcaint, déan an obair.
english: Never mind the talk, do the work.
literal: Don't mind the talk, do the work.

irish: Fanann an fear glic go mbíonn an ghaoth leis.
english: A clever person waits for the wind to be with them.
literal: The clever person waits until the wind is with them.

irish: Ná scaoil an gadhar ar na caoirigh.
english: Don't set the dog on the sheep.
literal: Don't release the dog on the sheep.

irish: Bíonn drochthús ag cúrsaí a éiríonn go maith.
english: Things that turn out well often have a bad start.
literal: Affairs that succeed often have a bad beginning.

irish: Caith uait an t-eiteach agus beidh do lámha saor.
english: Cast away refusal and your hands will be free.
literal: Cast from you the refusal and your hands will be free.

irish: Ná bris do bhabhta nó do chara.
english: Don't break your turn or your friend.
literal: Don't break your turn or your friend.

irish: Ní bhíonn rath ar shliocht an éithigh.
english: The offspring of lies never prosper.
literal: The descendants of a lie do not prosper.

irish: Tá dhá thaobh ar gach scéal.
english: There are two sides to every story.
literal: There are two sides to every story.

irish: An áit a mbíonn mná bíonn caint, is an áit a mbíonn géanna bíonn callán.
english: Where there are women there is talk, and where there are geese there is clamour.
literal: Where women are, there is talk; where geese are, there is noise.

irish: Ná caith do sheile in aghaidh na gaoithe.
english: Don't spit against the wind.
literal: Don't throw your spit against the wind.

irish: Ní haon mhaith a bheith ag caint gan éisteacht.
english: There is no point in talking without listening.
literal: There is no good in talking without listening.

irish: Má bhíonn tú ag siúl ar uisce, déan deifir.
english: If you are walking on water, make haste.
literal: If you are walking on water, hurry.

irish: Is treise an cleachtadh ná an chaint.
english: Practice is stronger than talk.
literal: Practice is stronger than talk.

irish: Ní bheireann mórán daoine buíochas as an méid nach bhfuil acu.
english: Not many people are grateful for what they don't have.
literal: Not many people give thanks for what they don't have.

irish: Tabhair grá don duine ach ná tabhair grá don locht.
english: Love the person but don't love the fault.
literal: Give love to the person but don't give love to the fault.

irish: Cuireann brón daoine le chéile.
english: Sorrow brings people together.
literal: Sorrow puts people together.

irish: Ní fhaca aon duine riamh ach leath an mhargaidh.
english: No one ever saw the whole of any bargain.
literal: No one ever saw but half the bargain.

irish: Is fearr an dea-chlú ná an t-ór.
english: A good name is better than gold.
literal: A good reputation is better than gold.

irish: Ná habair gach a gcloisfeá.
english: Don't say everything you hear.
literal: Don't say all that you would hear.

irish: Cloíonn mórán beagán.
english: Many a little makes a lot.
literal: Many a little overwhelms.

irish: Ní fhilleann solas an lae ach uair amháin.
english: The light of day returns only once.
literal: The light of the day returns but once.

irish: Is fearr go mall ná go brách.
english: Better late than never.
literal: Better late than forever (never).

irish: Maireann an chraobh ar an bhfál ach ní mhaireann an lámh do chuir í.
english: The branch lives on the hedge but the hand that planted it does not.
literal: The branch lives on the hedge but the hand that planted it does not live.

irish: Ní bhíonn tráth na féile ag an bhfear bocht.
english: The poor man has no time for feasting.
literal: The poor man does not have the time of the feast.

irish: Is fearr lá fliuch ná lá marbh.
english: A wet day is better than a dead day.
literal: A wet day is better than a dead day.

irish: Ní hé an té is airde glór is fearr an cás.
english: The loudest voice does not make the best argument.
literal: It is not the one with the loudest voice that has the best case.

irish: Cuireann spéir dhearg ar maidin drochshaol i gcuimhne.
english: A red sky in the morning recalls bad weather.
literal: A red sky in the morning brings bad weather to mind.

irish: Níl saoi gan locht.
english: There is no wise man without a fault.
literal: There is no sage without a fault.

irish: Is fearr bheith beo ar d'arán féin ná bheith marbh ar arán do chomharsan.
english: It is better to live on your own bread than to die on your neighbour's.
literal: It is better to be alive on your own bread than dead on your neighbour's bread.

irish: Bíonn fonn mór ar an bhfear atá dall ar an mbealach.
english: The man who is blind to the path has great desire.
literal: The man blind to the path has great desire.

irish: Ní théann aon duine chuig na cnoic ach uair amháin.
english: No one goes to the hills but once.
literal: No one goes to the hills but once.

irish: Ní neart go cur le chéile.
english: There is no strength without unity.
literal: There is no strength until you put it together.

irish: Is fearr Gaeilge briste ná Béarla cliste.
english: Broken Irish is better than clever English.
literal: Broken Irish is better than clever English.

irish: Ar scáth a chéile a mhaireann na daoine.
english: People live in each other's shelter.
literal: In the shadow of each other people live.

irish: Ní bhíonn an rath ach mar a mbíonn an smacht.
english: There is no prosperity without discipline.
literal: Prosperity only exists where there is order.

irish: Is maith an t-anlann an t-ocras.
english: Hunger is a good sauce.
literal: Hunger is good seasoning.

irish: Mol an óige agus tiocfaidh sí.
english: Praise the young and they will flourish.
literal: Praise the young and they will come.

irish: Ní hé lá na gaoithe lá na scolb.
english: A windy day is not the day for thatching.
literal: The day of the wind is not the day for scallops.

irish: Bíonn súil le muir ach ní bhíonn súil le tír.
english: There is hope from the sea but not from the grave.
literal: There is hope from the sea but no hope from the land.

irish: Is fearr rith maith ná drochsheasamh.
english: A good run is better than a bad stand.
literal: A good run is better than a bad standing.

irish: Maireann croí éadrom i bhfad.
english: A light heart lives long.
literal: A light heart lives long.

irish: An rud is annamh is iontach.
english: What is seldom is wonderful.
literal: The thing that is rare is wonderful.

irish: Ná déan nós agus ná bris nós.
english: Don't make a habit and don't break a habit.
literal: Don't make a custom and don't break a custom.

irish: Is minic a bhris béal duine a shrón.
english: Many a time a man's mouth broke his nose.
literal: Often a person's mouth broke their nose.

irish: Filleann an feall ar an bhfeallaire.
english: Treachery returns to the traitor.
literal: The treachery returns to the traitor.

irish: Giorraíonn beirt bóthar.
english: Two shorten the road.
literal: Two people shorten a road.

irish: Is fada an bóthar nach mbíonn casadh ann.
english: It's a long road that has no turning.
literal: It is a long road that has no turn in it.

irish: Nuair a bhíonn an cat amuigh, bíonn na lucha ag rince.
english: When the cat is out, the mice dance.
literal: When the cat is outside, the mice are dancing.

irish: Is treise tuile ná trua.
english: A flood is stronger than pity.
literal: Flood is stronger than pity.

irish: Aithníonn ciaróg ciaróg eile.
english: A beetle recognises another beetle.
literal: A beetle recognises another beetle.

irish: Ní féidir leat suí ar dhá chapall ag an am céanna.
english: You cannot sit on two horses at the same time.
literal: You cannot sit on two horses at the same time.

irish: Is fearr an tsláinte ná na táinte.
english: Health is better than wealth.
literal: Health is better than herds.

irish: Bíonn gach tosach lag.
english: Every beginning is weak.
literal: Every beginning is weak.

irish: Is maith an scáthán súil carad.
english: A friend's eye is a good mirror.
literal: A friend's eye is a good mirror.

irish: Ná bí ar an gcéad le nuacht a thabhairt.
english: Don't be first to bring news.
literal: Don't be the first to bring news.

irish: Castar na daoine ar a chéile ach ní castar na cnoic.
english: People meet but hills do not.
literal: People are turned towards each other but hills are not turned.

irish: Ní thagann ciall roimh aois.
english: Wisdom doesn't come before age.
literal: Sense does not come before age.

irish: Bíonn adharca fada ar na ba thar lear.
english: Far-away cows have long horns.
literal: Cows overseas have long horns.

irish: Ní féidir an t-arán a ithe agus a bheith agat.
english: You cannot eat your bread and have it.
literal: You cannot eat the bread and have it.

irish: Is mó cleas ná neart.
english: Cleverness is more than strength.
literal: There is more trick than strength.

irish: Tugann gach éan grá dá nead féin.
english: Every bird loves its own nest.
literal: Every bird gives love to its own nest.

irish: Ná cuir do mhéar idir an adhmad agus an rinn.
english: Don't put your finger between the wood and the blade.
literal: Don't put your finger between the wood and the point.

irish: Is buaine bladhm ná brí.
english: A blaze lasts longer than substance.
literal: A blaze is more lasting than substance.

irish: Ní chuireann an saol ach cuid dó féin air féin.
english: The world only puts its own share on itself.
literal: The world only puts some of itself on itself.

irish: Beatha duine a thoil.
english: A person's life is their will.
literal: A person's life is their desire.

irish: Is maith an rud iad cúig mhinicíochtaí.
english: The five senses are a good thing.
literal: Five frequencies are a good thing.

irish: Ná bris do loirgín ar stól nach bhfuil i do shlí.
english: Don't break your shin on a stool that isn't in your way.
literal: Don't break your shin on a stool not in your path.

irish: Is fusa caint ná obair.
english: Talk is easier than work.
literal: Talk is easier than work.

irish: Ní maith an gaoith nach séideann do dhuine éigin.
english: It's an ill wind that blows no one good.
literal: It is not a good wind that blows for no one.

irish: Éist le fuaim na habhann agus gheobhaidh tú breac.
english: Listen to the sound of the river and you will catch a trout.
literal: Listen to the sound of the river and you will get a trout.

irish: Ná déan deifir, déan é ceart.
english: Don't hurry, do it right.
literal: Don't make haste, do it right.

irish: Is fearr focal sa chúirt ná punt sa sparán.
english: A word in court is worth more than a pound in a purse.
literal: A word in court is better than a pound in a purse.

irish: Ní thagann gach maidin ach uair amháin.
english: Every morning comes only once.
literal: Every morning only comes once.

irish: Ní mar a shíltear a bhítear.
english: Things are not always as they seem.
literal: It is not as one thinks that one is.

irish: Is maith an bhail ar an gcaint é.
english: Actions speak louder than words.
literal: Good fortune comes from action not words.

irish: Ní fheicfidh súil leath oiread le dhá shúil.
english: One eye sees less than two.
literal: One eye won't see half as much as two eyes.

irish: An rud nach gcuala tú, ná habair é.
english: Don't repeat what you haven't heard.
literal: The thing you did not hear, don't say it.

irish: Ní éiríonn an ghrian ar son amadáin amháin.
english: The sun does not rise for one fool alone.
literal: The sun does not rise for the sake of one fool.

irish: Ní bhíonn an t-ádh i gcónaí ar an gcaor chéanna.
english: Luck is not always on the same bush.
literal: Luck is not always on the same berry bush.

irish: Is fearr cara sa chúirt ná punt sa sparán.
english: A friend in court is better than a pound in a purse.
literal: A friend in court is better than a pound in a purse.

irish: Imíonn na bliana is fanann na laethanta.
english: The years go but the days remain.
literal: The years go away and the days stay.

irish: Is minic a bhí cú mall sona.
english: A slow hound was often lucky.
literal: Often the slow hound was lucky.

irish: Ná caith do chuid airgid ar rud nach bhfuil agat.
english: Don't spend money you don't have.
literal: Don't spend your money on a thing you don't have.

irish: Is cosúil le hobair mhaith an dua mór.
english: Great effort looks like good work.
literal: Great effort is like good work.

irish: Is cuma leis an gcat cé acu a bhíonn sé ar an gclár nó faoin gclár.
english: The cat doesn't mind whether it's on or under the shelf.
literal: The cat doesn't care whether it is on the board or under the board.

irish: Níor dhún Dia doras riamh nar oscail sé ceann eile.
english: God never closed one door without opening another.
literal: God never closed a door without opening another one.

irish: Is glas iad na cnoic i bhfad uainn.
english: The hills far away are green.
literal: The hills that are far from us are green.

irish: Bíonn caora dhubh ar an tréad is gile.
english: There is a black sheep in the whitest flock.
literal: A black sheep is in the brightest flock.

irish: Is fearr an tsláinte ná an táinte.
english: Good health is better than wealth.
literal: Health is better than herds of cattle.

irish: Ceol gan amhrán mar mháistir gan scoil.
english: Music without song is like a master without a school.
literal: Music without song is like a master without a school.

irish: Is fearr greim de ghiorria ná dhá ghreim de chat.
english: A taste of hare is better than two tastes of cat.
literal: A bite of hare is better than two bites of cat.

irish: Cuir uait é nó cuir leat é.
english: Either put it from you or take it with you.
literal: Put it from you or put it with you.

irish: Gach tosach lag is gach deireadh láidir.
english: Every beginning is weak and every ending is strong.
literal: Every beginning weak and every end strong.

irish: Ná lig do rún le fear óg ná le páiste.
english: Don't share your secret with a young man or a child.
literal: Don't let your secret to a young man or to a child.

irish: Is iomaí cor sa tsaol.
english: Life has many twists.
literal: There are many turns in life.

irish: Nuair is treise an cat, tosaíonn sé ag ithe.
english: When the cat is strongest it begins to eat.
literal: When the cat is strongest, it starts eating.

irish: Is fearr bheith i measc na ndaoine ná bheith aonarach.
english: It is better to be among people than to be alone.
literal: It is better to be among the people than to be alone.

irish: Caith do bholgam is ná bain torann as.
english: Take your mouthful and make no noise about it.
literal: Throw your mouthful and make no noise from it.

irish: Ní fhanann trá le fear mall.
english: The ebb tide waits for no one.
literal: The ebb tide does not wait for a slow man.

irish: Cuir síoda ar ghabhar is is gabhar i gcónaí é.
english: Put silk on a goat and it's still a goat.
literal: Put silk on a goat and it is a goat still.

irish: Bíonn an rath i gceann na mbeart.
english: Luck is at the head of deeds.
literal: Prosperity is at the head of deeds.

irish: Tuar drochaimsire an ghrian bheith dearg tráthnóna.
english: A red sun at evening foretells bad weather.
literal: A red sun in the evening is an omen of bad weather.

irish: Ná caith do chuid ama ag caint faoin obair, déan í.
english: Don't waste your time talking about work, do it.
literal: Don't spend your time talking about the work, do it.

irish: Creid ach fíoraigh.
english: Believe but verify.
literal: Believe but confirm.

irish: Ní thuigeann an sách an seang.
english: The well-fed does not understand the hungry.
literal: The satiated does not understand the lean.

irish: Is cóir do gach duine a bhróg féin a chaitheamh.
english: Everyone should wear their own shoe.
literal: It is right for every person to wear their own shoe.

irish: Níl aon tintéan mar do thintéan féin.
english: There's no fireside like your own fireside.
literal: There is no fireside like your own fireside.

irish: Bíodh foighne agat agus gheobhaidh tú do mhian.
english: Have patience and you will get your desire.
literal: Have patience and you will get your wish.

irish: Tá sé chomh fada le Lá Samhraidh gan aon oíche.
english: It is as long as a summer's day without night.
literal: It is as long as a summer's day without any night.

irish: Is minic a bhíonn an fhírinne searbh.
english: The truth is often bitter.
literal: The truth is often bitter.

irish: Más maith leat dul i bhfad, ná gabh leat do lámh fholamh.
english: If you want to go far, don't go empty-handed.
literal: If you like to go far, don't take an empty hand with you.

irish: Bíonn an duine go minic mar a bhíonn a chuideachta.
english: A person is often like the company they keep.
literal: A person is often as their company is.

irish: Aithnítear cara i gcruachás.
english: A friend is known in hardship.
literal: A friend is recognized in difficulty.

irish: Is maith an rud é an t-airgead a bheith agat ach níl maith dá laghad ann mura gcaitheann tú é.
english: Money is good to have but worthless if you don't spend it.
literal: Money is a good thing to have but it's no use if you don't spend it.

irish: Ní mar a cheaptar a tharlaíonn.
english: Things don't always happen as expected.
literal: It doesn't happen as it is thought.

irish: Gach uile dhuine ar a lámh féin.
english: Every man for himself.
literal: Every person on their own hand.

irish: Más fada an lá tagann an tráthnóna.
english: However long the day, the evening comes.
literal: Even if the day is long, the evening comes.

irish: Ní dhíolann dearmad fiacha.
english: Forgetting doesn't pay debts.
literal: Forgetting does not pay debts.

irish: Fógraíonn an éan a theacht féin.
english: A bird announces its own arrival.
literal: The bird announces its own coming.

irish: Is fearr troid mhaith ná aon drochshíocháin.
english: A good fight is better than a bad peace.
literal: A good fight is better than any bad peace.

irish: Ní bhíonn an fear maith gan a locht.
english: Even a good man has his faults.
literal: The good man is not without his fault.

irish: Déan beagán agus déan é maith.
english: Do little and do it well.
literal: Do a little and do it well.

irish: Bíonn an saol ag éirí níos fearr i gcónaí.
english: Life is always getting better.
literal: Life is always getting better.

irish: Inis dom cé leis a mbíonn tú is inseoidh mé duit cé thú féin.
english: Tell me who you walk with and I'll tell you who you are.
literal: Tell me who you are with and I will tell you who you yourself are.

irish: Is fearr deireadh maith ná tús maith.
english: A good ending is better than a good beginning.
literal: A good end is better than a good start.

irish: Ní féidir an ghaoth a choinneáil i bpóca.
english: You cannot keep the wind in a pocket.
literal: The wind cannot be kept in a pocket.

irish: An té nach gcuireann san earrach ní bhaineann sé san fhómhar.
english: He who does not sow in spring will not reap in autumn.
literal: The one who does not sow in spring does not reap in autumn.

irish: Ná bris an chiúin go mbrisfear ort í.
english: Don't break the peace until it's broken upon you.
literal: Don't break the peace until it is broken on you.

irish: Más maith leat an tsíocháin, ullmhaigh don chogadh.
english: If you want peace, prepare for war.
literal: If you like peace, prepare for war.

irish: Is leor nod don eolach.
english: A word to the wise is enough.
literal: A nod is enough for the knowledgeable person.

irish: Is fearr an tsimplíocht ná an chlíste.
english: Simplicity is better than cleverness.
literal: Simplicity is better than cleverness.

irish: Ná cuir an méar sa tine mura mbeidh tú sásta le dó.
english: Don't put your finger in the fire unless you're willing to be burnt.
literal: Don't put the finger in the fire if you won't be happy with a burn.

irish: Bíonn siúlach scéalach.
english: A traveller has many tales.
literal: One who travels is full of stories.

irish: Má tá an hata ort caith é.
english: If the hat fits, wear it.
literal: If the hat is on you, wear it.

irish: Cuireann áthas croí áilleacht ar an aghaidh.
english: A joyful heart makes a beautiful face.
literal: Joy of heart puts beauty on the face.

irish: Is é an spéir an teorainn.
english: The sky is the limit.
literal: The sky is the boundary.

irish: Tús maith leath na hoibre.
english: A good start is half the work.
literal: A good beginning is half the work.

irish: Ní hionann inniu agus inné.
english: Today is not the same as yesterday.
literal: Today and yesterday are not the same.

irish: Ná téigh i muinín mada gan fhiacail.
english: Don't trust a dog without teeth.
literal: Don't rely on a dog without teeth.

irish: Is fearr cosc ná leigheas.
english: Prevention is better than cure.
literal: Prevention is better than remedy.

irish: An rud a bhíonn sa chnámh, bíonn sé sa tsmior.
english: What's in the bone is in the marrow.
literal: What is in the bone is in the marrow.

irish: Más é do thoil é, is é do cheol é.
english: What you desire is your music.
literal: If it is your will, it is your music.

irish: Ná déan obair an lae amáraigh inniu.
english: Don't do tomorrow's work today.
literal: Don't do tomorrow's work today.

irish: Bíonn gach duine lách go dtéann bó ina gharraí.
english: Everyone is pleasant until a cow gets into their garden.
literal: Everyone is nice until a cow goes into their garden.

irish: Is maith an t-anlann an t-ocras.
english: Hunger is a good sauce.
literal: Hunger is good seasoning.

irish: Ní thiocfaidh an lá nach dtiocfaidh a oíche féin.
english: There is no day that won't bring its own night.
literal: No day will come that won't bring its own night.

irish: Nuair a bhíonn an fhírinne i gceist, ná bíodh eagla ort.
english: When truth is at stake, have no fear.
literal: When truth is involved, don't be afraid.

irish: Ní bhíonn bua gan donas.
english: There is no victory without some loss.
literal: There is no victory without misfortune.

irish: Is iomaí malairt intinne a tharlaíonn idir lámha.
english: Many a change of mind happens in the doing.
literal: Many a change of mind happens between the hands.

irish: Bíonn an saol mar shnámh in aghaidh easa.
english: Life is like swimming against a waterfall.
literal: Life is like swimming against a waterfall.

irish: Is buaine clú ná saol.
english: Reputation outlasts life.
literal: Fame is more lasting than life.

irish: Ní thagann feabhas ar obair leis an deifir.
english: Haste does not improve the work.
literal: Improvement in work doesn't come with haste.

irish: Ní féidir leis an mbeach mil a dhéanamh gan bláthanna.
english: The bee cannot make honey without flowers.
literal: The bee cannot make honey without flowers.

irish: Is maith an giolla an t-am.
english: Time is a good servant.
literal: Time is a good helper.

irish: Beir ar an deis agus ná lig uait í.
english: Seize the opportunity and don't let it go.
literal: Take the opportunity and don't let it from you.

irish: Ná bíodh do theanga faoi do chrios.
english: Don't let your tongue be under your belt.
literal: Don't have your tongue under your belt.

irish: An rud nach bhfuil i ndán duit, ní bhfaighidh tú é.
english: What is not fated for you, you will not get.
literal: The thing not destined for you, you will not get.

irish: Is leigheas ar gach olc an foighne.
english: Patience is a cure for every ill.
literal: Patience is a cure for every evil.

irish: Scéal ar bith níos fearr ná drochscéal.
english: No news is better than bad news.
literal: No story is better than a bad story.

irish: Ní gach uile lá a bhíonn an fharraige socair.
english: Not every day is the sea calm.
literal: Not every day is the sea at rest.

irish: Bíonn an mhaith agus an t-olc ag dul le chéile.
english: Good and evil go together.
literal: Good and evil go with each other.

irish: Is mó rud le foghlaim ná mar is féidir a mhúineadh.
english: There is more to learn than can ever be taught.
literal: There are more things to learn than can be taught.

irish: Féach sular léime tú.
english: Look before you leap.
literal: Look before you jump.

irish: Is maith an ceol an obair.
english: Work is good music.
literal: Work is good music.

irish: Ná déan cúl le cine.
english: Don't turn your back on your own people.
literal: Don't turn a back to your kin.

irish: Is fearr beagán den ghaol ná mórán den charthanas.
english: A little kinship is better than much charity.
literal: A little of kinship is better than a lot of charity.

irish: Más glas iad na cnoic i bhfad uait, is glas do gort féin freisin.
english: If the distant hills are green, so too is your own field.
literal: If the hills far from you are green, your own field is green too.

irish: Is é an duine ciallmhar nach ndéanann gach rud is féidir leis a dhéanamh.
english: A wise person does not do everything they are capable of.
literal: The sensible person is who does not do all they can do.

irish: Is cuma faoin gcaint, déan an obair.
english: Never mind the talk, do the work.
literal: Don't mind the talk, do the work.

irish: Fanann an fear glic go mbíonn an ghaoth leis.
english: A clever person waits for the wind to be with them.
literal: The clever person waits until the wind is with them.

irish: Ná scaoil an gadhar ar na caoirigh.
english: Don't set the dog on the sheep.
literal: Don't release the dog on the sheep.

irish: Bíonn drochthús ag cúrsaí a éiríonn go maith.
english: Things that turn out well often have a bad start.
literal: Affairs that succeed often have a bad beginning.

irish: Caith uait an t-eiteach agus beidh do lámha saor.
english: Cast away refusal and your hands will be free.
literal: Cast from you the refusal and your hands will be free.

irish: Ná bris do bhabhta nó do chara.
english: Don't break your turn or your friend.
literal: Don't break your turn or your friend.

irish: Ní bhíonn rath ar shliocht an éithigh.
english: The offspring of lies never prosper.
literal: The descendants of a lie do not prosper.

irish: Tá dhá thaobh ar gach scéal.
english: There are two sides to every story.
literal: There are two sides to every story.

irish: An áit a mbíonn mná bíonn caint, is an áit a mbíonn géanna bíonn callán.
english: Where there are women there is talk, and where there are geese there is clamour.
literal: Where women are, there is talk; where geese are, there is noise.

irish: Ná caith do sheile in aghaidh na gaoithe.
english: Don't spit against the wind.
literal: Don't throw your spit against the wind.

irish: Ní haon mhaith a bheith ag caint gan éisteacht.
english: There is no point in talking without listening.
literal: There is no good in talking without listening.

irish: Má bhíonn tú ag siúl ar uisce, déan deifir.
english: If you are walking on water, make haste.
literal: If you are walking on water, hurry.

irish: Is treise an cleachtadh ná an chaint.
english: Practice is stronger than talk.
literal: Practice is stronger than talk.

irish: Ní bheireann mórán daoine buíochas as an méid nach bhfuil acu.
english: Not many people are grateful for what they don't have.
literal: Not many people give thanks for what they don't have.

irish: Tabhair grá don duine ach ná tabhair grá don locht.
english: Love the person but don't love the fault.
literal: Give love to the person but don't give love to the fault.

irish: Cuireann brón daoine le chéile.
english: Sorrow brings people together.
literal: Sorrow puts people together.

irish: Ní fhaca aon duine riamh ach leath an mhargaidh.
english: No one ever saw the whole of any bargain.
literal: No one ever saw but half the bargain.

irish: Is fearr an dea-chlú ná an t-ór.
english: A good name is better than gold.
literal: A good reputation is better than gold.

irish: Ná habair gach a gcloisfeá.
english: Don't say everything you hear.
literal: Don't say all that you would hear.

irish: Cloíonn mórán beagán.
english: Many a little makes a lot.
literal: Many a little overwhelms.

irish: Ní fhilleann solas an lae ach uair amháin.
english: The light of day returns only once.
literal: The light of the day returns but once.

irish: Is fearr go mall ná go brách.
english: Better late than never.
literal: Better late than forever (never).

irish: Maireann an chraobh ar an bhfál ach ní mhaireann an lámh do chuir í.
english: The branch lives on the hedge but the hand that planted it does not.
literal: The branch lives on the hedge but the hand that planted it does not live.

irish: Ní bhíonn tráth na féile ag an bhfear bocht.
english: The poor man has no time for feasting.
literal: The poor man does not have the time of the feast.

irish: Is fearr lá fliuch ná lá marbh.
english: A wet day is better than a dead day.
literal: A wet day is better than a dead day.

irish: Ní hé an té is airde glór is fearr an cás.
english: The loudest voice does not make the best argument.
literal: It is not the one with the loudest voice that has the best case.

irish: Cuireann spéir dhearg ar maidin drochshaol i gcuimhne.
english: A red sky in the morning recalls bad weather.
literal: A red sky in the morning brings bad weather to mind.

irish: Níl saoi gan locht.
english: There is no wise man without a fault.
literal: There is no sage without a fault.

irish: Is fearr bheith beo ar d'arán féin ná bheith marbh ar arán do chomharsan.
english: It is better to live on your own bread than to die on your neighbour's.
literal: It is better to be alive on your own bread than dead on your neighbour's bread.

irish: Bíonn fonn mór ar an bhfear atá dall ar an mbealach.
english: The man who is blind to the path has great desire.
literal: The man blind to the path has great desire.

irish: Ní théann aon duine chuig na cnoic ach uair amháin.
english: No one goes to the hills but once.
literal: No one goes to the hills but once.

irish: Ní neart go cur le chéile.
english: There is no strength without unity.
literal: There is no strength until you put it together.

irish: Is fearr Gaeilge briste ná Béarla cliste.
english: Broken Irish is better than clever English.
literal: Broken Irish is better than clever English.

irish: Ar scáth a chéile a mhaireann na daoine.
english: People live in each other's shelter.
literal: In the shadow of each other people live.

irish: Ní bhíonn an rath ach mar a mbíonn an smacht.
english: There is no prosperity without discipline.
literal: Prosperity only exists where there is order.

irish: Is maith an t-anlann an t-ocras.
english: Hunger is a good sauce.
literal: Hunger is good seasoning.

irish: Mol an óige agus tiocfaidh sí.
english: Praise the young and they will flourish.
literal: Praise the young and they will come.

irish: Ní hé lá na gaoithe lá na scolb.
english: A windy day is not the day for thatching.
literal: The day of the wind is not the day for scallops.

irish: Bíonn súil le muir ach ní bhíonn súil le tír.
english: There is hope from the sea but not from the grave.
literal: There is hope from the sea but no hope from the land.

irish: Is fearr rith maith ná drochsheasamh.
english: A good run is better than a bad stand.
literal: A good run is better than a bad standing.

irish: Maireann croí éadrom i bhfad.
english: A light heart lives long.
literal: A light heart lives long.

irish: An rud is annamh is iontach.
english: What is seldom is wonderful.
literal: The thing that is rare is wonderful.

irish: Ná déan nós agus ná bris nós.
english: Don't make a habit and don't break a habit.
literal: Don't make a custom and don't break a custom.

irish: Is minic a bhris béal duine a shrón.
english: Many a time a man's mouth broke his nose.
literal: Often a person's mouth broke their nose.

irish: Filleann an feall ar an bhfeallaire.
english: Treachery returns to the traitor.
literal: The treachery returns to the traitor.

irish: Giorraíonn beirt bóthar.
english: Two shorten the road.
literal: Two people shorten a road.

irish: Is fada an bóthar nach mbíonn casadh ann.
english: It's a long road that has no turning.
literal: It is a long road that has no turn in it.

irish: Nuair a bhíonn an cat amuigh, bíonn na lucha ag rince.
english: When the cat is out, the mice dance.
literal: When the cat is outside, the mice are dancing.

irish: Is treise tuile ná trua.
english: A flood is stronger than pity.
literal: Flood is stronger than pity.

irish: Aithníonn ciaróg ciaróg eile.
english: A beetle recognises another beetle.
literal: A beetle recognises another beetle.

irish: Ní féidir leat suí ar dhá chapall ag an am céanna.
english: You cannot sit on two horses at the same time.
literal: You cannot sit on two horses at the same time.

irish: Is fearr an tsláinte ná na táinte.
english: Health is better than wealth.
literal: Health is better than herds.

irish: Bíonn gach tosach lag.
english: Every beginning is weak.
literal: Every beginning is weak.

irish: Is maith an scáthán súil carad.
english: A friend's eye is a good mirror.
literal: A friend's eye is a good mirror.

irish: Ná bí ar an gcéad le nuacht a thabhairt.
english: Don't be first to bring news.
literal: Don't be the first to bring news.

irish: Castar na daoine ar a chéile ach ní castar na cnoic.
english: People meet but hills do not.
literal: People are turned towards each other but hills are not turned.

irish: Ní thagann ciall roimh aois.
english: Wisdom doesn't come before age.
literal: Sense does not come before age.

irish: Bíonn adharca fada ar na ba thar lear.
english: Far-away cows have long horns.
literal: Cows overseas have long horns.

irish: Ní féidir an t-arán a ithe agus a bheith agat.
english: You cannot eat your bread and have it.
literal: You cannot eat the bread and have it.

irish: Is mó cleas ná neart.
english: Cleverness is more than strength.
literal: There is more trick than strength.
